title: 2026 Oscars: 5 takeaways from the night
text: The 2026 Oscars ceremony delivered several major takeaways. First, Paul Thomas Anderson's 25-year journey to his first Best Director Oscar captivated audiences, with his acceptance speech reflecting on the long road to recognition. Second, the tight Best Picture race between One Battle After Another and Sinners demonstrated how unpredictable the Academy can be despite statistical models suggesting clear frontrunners. Third, Michael B. Jordan's Best Actor victory for Sinners proved the strength of individual performances even when supporting films don't win the top prizes. Fourth, the emergence of new award categories, including Best Casting, reflected efforts to recognize additional contributions to filmmaking. Fifth, Ryan Coogler's strong showing with Sinners across multiple categories indicated evolving tastes within the Academy and potential for increased diversity in future winner selections. The ceremony also highlighted Warner Bros. dominance, as both Best Picture contenders came from the studio. Host Conan O'Brien's opening monologue and various presenters' comments reflected on contemporary political and social issues, emphasizing cinema's role in addressing pressing concerns.
